meta.title: Evidence Before Leaderboards
meta.subtitle: A leakage-aware comparison of MiT-B3 U-Net and nnU-Net with a negative contour-channel ablation
meta.author: IMP Project Research Report
meta.non_clinical_notice: Non-clinical research demo. Segmentation output is not a diagnosis.

slides.title: Evidence before leaderboard claims
slides.claim: A repaired evidence contract turns a contaminated benchmark and a failed hypothesis into defensible research.
body: MiT-B3 U-Net versus nnU-Net v2
Negative contour-channel ablation
Protected test remains sealed; this is not state of the art
notes: Open with the tension: a high Dice score is not useful when the evidence contract is broken.
State that this talk values what the evidence supports, including a negative result.
Do not describe the system as clinical or state of the art.

slides.title: Leakage changed the meaning of every favorable score
slides.claim: Three patient identities and 13 rows crossed legacy split boundaries, so the old leaderboard became historical evidence rather than a scientific ranking.
body: Patient overlap violates independent patient-level interpretation.
Old test-v2 values remain visible only as contamination-aware history.
The repair starts with identity groups, not model tuning.
notes: Explain that leakage is a scientific design defect, not a cosmetic caveat.
Do not compare legacy model scores as if they were independent test results.
Use this slide to motivate the evidence hierarchy.

slides.title: Two hypotheses, two evidence classes
slides.claim: The architecture comparison and the contour-channel ablation answer different questions and must not be collapsed into one ranking.
body: RQ1: How do complete MiT-B3 and nnU-Net systems trade overlap, boundary quality, precision, and recall?
RQ2: Does a saliency-constrained contour channel improve a matched MiT-B3 control?
Decision rule: reject claims that exceed their partition, seed, or metric contract.
notes: Keep the architecture comparison descriptive because it has one recorded run per system.
Describe Loop206 as a controlled train-screen ablation with three selected seeds.
Transition to the interactive pipeline as the map of the evidence flow.

slides.kicker: CLICK A MODULE TO INSPECT IT
slides.title: The claim can only be as strong as its weakest evidence boundary
slides.claim: Every model output passes through data, protocol, metric, and claim gates before it reaches the paper or demo.
body: Choose a module below.
Press Escape from any detail slide to return here.
notes: Use this slide nonlinearly: select the module that best matches the audience question.
For the default talk, visit modules from left to right.
The pipeline is a navigation spine, not a decorative architecture diagram.

slides.title: Clean-v3 repairs identity leakage before model comparison
slides.claim: The leakage-audited manifest contains 2,869 images with no detected cross-split identity-group overlap.
body: 2,008 training images
431 adaptive development-validation images
430 sealed test images
Identity, exact duplicate, perceptual duplicate, and split-group checks
notes: Say no detected overlap, not proof of no hidden biological relationship.
Clarify that validation was already opened and used adaptively.
The 430-row test partition remains sealed.

slides.title: The comparison is between complete systems, not isolated encoders
slides.claim: MiT-B3 uses a fixed preprocessing-aware 384x384 pipeline; nnU-Net v2 uses raw RGB and a self-configuring 256x256 system.
body: IMP control: LAB-luminance CLAHE, percentile stretch, median filter, MiT-B3 U-Net.
nnU-Net v2: raw RGB, generated plans, 2D self-configuring pipeline.
Resolution, decoder, loss, augmentation, and policy all differ.
The result cannot identify which component causes the observed difference.
notes: Avoid calling this a controlled architecture ablation.
Explain that nnU-Net is a strong system-level baseline.
Mention the historical geometry mismatch as a limitation, not a hidden footnote.

slides.title: nnU-Net records higher point estimates, not statistical superiority
slides.claim: On adaptive development-validation, nnU-Net v2 has higher robust Dice and boundary F1 point estimates; MiT-B3 retains slightly higher precision.
body: No paired confidence interval is available.
Validation informed iterative development and promotion decisions.
The correct conclusion is descriptive and contract-specific.
notes: Read the values as recorded point estimates.
Do not say statistically significant, superior, or test performance.
Use the precision direction to show that metric trade-offs matter.

slides.title: Loop206 isolates one intervention: the fourth input channel
slides.claim: Control and candidate share architecture, preprocessing, seeds, budget, and loss; only the zero versus contour channel changes.
body: 308 fit groups; 76 train-screen holdout groups
Three selected seeds and three preregistered views
20 epochs per matched arm
Cluster bootstrap resamples groups after averaging seeds and views
notes: This is the stronger causal comparison because the arms are matched.
The interval is conditional on the selected seeds and does not estimate seed-selection uncertainty.
No protected image or mask participates in fitting, thresholding, selection, or evaluation.

slides.title: The contour channel fails the primary gate
slides.claim: Seed-and-view-averaged robust Dice changes by -0.0313; its conditional 95% interval lies entirely below zero.
slides.evidence_label: train_screen | conditional on the selected seeds
body: Per-seed directions are mixed: one slightly positive, two negative.
The candidate is rejected before protected evaluation.
notes: Pause on the negative result: it is the main controlled finding.
Do not generalize from this binary contour mechanism to all boundary methods.
Explain that a failed preregistered gate prevents expensive protected-test fishing.

slides.title: The demo compares only what the registry authorizes
slides.claim: The public workbench supports exact fixed-cache comparison; arbitrary uploads remain control-only because candidate prior parity is 0/76.
body: Real GPU outputs and path-free receipts
Provider-bound ground truth with raw, decoded, and runtime mask digests
Candidate output disabled for arbitrary uploads
Non-clinical research demo; not a diagnosis
notes: Open the live demo only after local preflight passes.
Show fixed-cache evidence first, then explain why arbitrary candidate inference is locked.
Keep the non-clinical warning visible.

slides.title: Claims are reproducible; full retraining is not yet clone-runnable
slides.claim: The release binds claims, tables, figures, PDF, model IDs, and source reports by hash while explicitly disclosing missing experiment artifacts.
slides.evidence_label: hash-bound release | protected test remains sealed
body: Clean checkout CPU CI rebuilds tables, audits the paper, and compiles the PDF.
Runtime checkpoints and data remain outside Git and are verified at startup.
Loop191/192 configs, implementation modules, and paired predictions are incomplete in the tracked release.
Physical RTX 4060 and browser screenshots remain unverified.
notes: Separate claim reproducibility from full experiment reproduction.
State what is intentionally excluded from Git: data, checkpoints, caches, and secrets.
Do not imply that this machine's RTX 5060 Ti rehearsal verifies the physical laptop.

slides.title: A narrower conclusion is the stronger scientific result
slides.claim: nnU-Net records higher development-validation point estimates, the hard contour prior fails its matched train-screen gate, and no protected-test or state-of-the-art claim is authorized.
body: Repair leakage before optimizing models.
Use strong system baselines without hiding contract differences.
Publish negative ablations when they reject a mechanism.
Next: reproduce both systems under one geometry contract before any protected test.
notes: Resolve the opening tension: evidence discipline prevented a false leaderboard claim.
End on the next defensible experiment, not a generic thank-you slide.
Invite questions about evidence boundaries, the matched ablation, or the demo.
